summary: Agent Coordination Workflow - 6 scenarios covering: architect handoff, E2E test generation, coder TDD cycle, parser verification, registry matching, and executor results
instructions: Read the feature file at features/agent-coordination.feature. It contains 6 scenarios describing the agent coordination workflow. Your job is to write executable Go acceptance tests that implement these scenarios as step definitions. Create the tests in internal/acceptance/agent_coordination_test.go. Each Gherkin step should map to a step definition function. The tests should use the gherkin parser, runner registry, executor, and handoff packages already built in internal/. After writing the acceptance tests, verify they compile and run (they should fail since not all production code is in place). Then create agent_context/handoff-to-coder.json and notify the Coder agent to begin TDD.
